events.event_description: Diplomatic relations established with the Federation of Central American States when President Monroe received Antonio José Cañaz as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. No resident mission yet established.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations were established on August 4, 1824, when President James Monroe received Antonio José Cañaz as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary on August 4, 1824.

events.event_description: American Legation established in Guatemala when Chargé d'Affaires John Williams presented his credentials to the Federation of Central American States.

evidence.quote: Chargé d'Affaires John Williams presented his credentials to the Federation of Central American States on May 3, 1826.

events.event_description: Diplomatic relations re-established with independent Guatemala when Chargé d'Affaires Elijah Hise presented his credentials to the Republic of Guatemala.

evidence.quote: Chargé d'Affaires Elijah Hise presented his credentials to the Republic of Guatemala on or shortly before January 21, 1849.

events.event_description: American Legation in Guatemala raised to Embassy status when Henry C. Hall presented his credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary.

evidence.quote: The American Legation in Guatemala was raised to Embassy status when Henry C. Hall, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, presented his credentials on August 10, 1882.
